g.text: Centralized Redis Counter: every Request pays +1 RTT
Every gateway calls INCR on the same Redis key before serving the request.
Counts are exact globally; the cost is +1 RTT on every request, and Redis becomes a SPOF + scaling bottleneck.
1,000,000 req/sec at the edge → 1,000,000 INCR/sec on Redis → Redis CPU is the cluster's actual limit, not the backend's.
Mitigations: Redis Cluster (shard the counter key), batched INCRs every 100 ms per gateway, or fall back to gossip-based local counting.
